title: Dainty Peppery Malaysian Chicken Spring-Rolls
description: A high-protein, peppery Malaysian-inspired snack using crisp phyllo dough and blanched vegetables for a light post-workout recovery meal.
instructions: Blanch the shredded cabbage and julienned carrots in boiling water for sixty seconds until slightly tender.
Drain the vegetables thoroughly and squeeze out any excess moisture with a clean cloth.
Saute the minced garlic and ground chicken in a non-stick pan until the meat is fully cooked.
Stir in the cracked white pepper and soy sauce to season the chicken mixture.
Mix the blanched vegetables with the peppery chicken and drizzle with sesame oil.
Lay out one sheet of phyllo dough and spray lightly with olive oil before folding it in half.
Place two tablespoons of the filling at the bottom edge of the dough and roll tightly while tucking in the sides.
Repeat the process until all filling is used and place rolls on a parchment-lined baking sheet.
Bake the spring rolls at 200 degrees Celsius for fifteen minutes until the pastry is golden and crisp.
Serve the warm rolls immediately with a side of light dipping sauce.
